moderators_tested: mean_age; publication_year; sample_size; study_quality; monetary_reward; task_version; region
boundary_rationale: All 110 studies used the standard 100-trial IGT with healthy naive participants. Per the pre-specified coding rubric (zanini2025_coding_rubric.md Step 3), the standard IGT with naive participants maps to dot-stage processing: reactive responding to immediate outcomes with no systematic integration. No studies included enriched feedback (network) or explicit rule cues / analytical samples (linear). Because all studies occupy a single DLN stage, there is no between-stage variation to serve as a moderator. DLN therefore predicts no heterogeneity reduction from stage coding in this dataset. This prediction is consistent with Zanini et al.'s finding that none of seven tested moderators resolved the significant between-study heterogeneity (Q=206.001, p<0.001, tau2=20.782). The residual heterogeneity reflects within-stage variability (e.g., individual differences in somatic marker sensitivity, sample composition, cultural factors) that DLN stage coding is not designed to explain. This dataset constitutes a coherent boundary condition: DLN predicts moderation only when task demands or sample characteristics create between-stage variation.
